creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon 26 mar 07 at 6:36:38 +0200, Mikhail Teterin <mi+kde@aldan.algebra.com>
  wrote:
  
 > The errors are due to something in how newer jails handle permissions. All of 
 > the self-tests were passing fine, when the automatic tests were introduced -- 
 > the port itself has not changed since then...
 > 
 > Simply disabling the automatic self-tests, as the proposed patch is doing, is 
 > NOT a "fix"...
 
 The tests are not failing, and I don't propose to disable them, but just
 to move them to the regression-test target (which is automatically
 launched by pointyhat or tinderbox).
 
 The failure is when the port rebuilds man pages, and it is addressed by
 patch-af.
 
 Regards,
 -- 
 Th. Thomas.
